data.title: Fed Chair Powell held Monetary Policy firm, no September rate cut decided. Interest rates held steady at 4.25%-4.50%, but two members supported a cut. Powell resists pressure, emphasizes need to prevent embedded price increases. Decision on rate cut to be made after jobs and inflation data in September.
data.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell reaffirmed the central bank's stance as "modestly restrictive" and stated no decision has been made regarding a rate cut in September. The Federal Reserve maintained interest rates at 4.25%-4.50%, with two members advocating for a rate cut, making it the most divided meeting in 30 years. Powell highlighted inflation slightly above 2% and stressed the need for policy to remain tight to prevent price increases from getting embedded. Powell resisted pressures from markets and the Trump administration for rate cuts, citing the need for more jobs and inflation data before making any decisions. There is uncertainty surrounding the Fed's next move, which will be influenced by upcoming data and inflation trends. Powell emphasized the importance of timing in order to effectively manage inflation without causing unnecessary economic damage.

data.title: 192,000 play kitchens recalled due to strangulation risk; child dies. Hook on toy kitchen poses asphyxiation hazard.
data.description: The U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission has reported that children playing with the toy kitchen set may be at risk of getting ensnared on a hook, leading to potential asphyxiation. Parents should be cautious as incidents like these can result in serious harm. It is crucial to pay attention to such warnings, as the safety of children is paramount. It is imperative to check toys for any hazards and ensure they are suitable for use by young children. Take necessary precautions and keep an eye on children during playtime to avoid any accidents. Stay informed and vigilant to prevent any unfortunate incidents.

data.title: The stock market had its toughest week in months as renewed economic fears were triggered by concerning U.S. labor data and new tariffs, affecting global markets.
data.description: The recent release of data revealing weaknesses in the U.S. labor market, combined with President Trump's implementation of another round of tariffs, caused significant concern among global investors. This led to a decline in stock prices, the value of the dollar, and other financial indicators worldwide.

data.title: Trump alleges manipulated job stats, plans to dismiss Labor official, risking trust in government economic data.
data.description: Economists are concerned that removing the leader of the Bureau of Labor Statistics may shake trust in government economic data. This move could potentially impact the reliability of key economic indicators and statistics, leading to uncertainties among stakeholders. The Bureau of Labor Statistics plays a crucial role in collecting and analyzing data such as employment numbers and inflation rates, which are utilized by policymakers, businesses, and the general public. Any disruptions in the leadership of this institution could raise doubts about the accuracy and transparency of economic information provided by the government. It is essential to maintain stability and credibility within the Bureau of Labor Statistics to ensure the continued integrity of economic data.

data.title: PagerDuty (NYSE:PD) named Leader in the 2025 GigaOm Radar for AIOps for 4th year, focused on innovation and AI-centric approach.
data.description: PagerDuty, a prominent player in digital operations management, has received recognition in the 2025 GigaOm Radar for AIOps, marking the fourth consecutive year of being named a Leader and Outperformer in the field. The report places PagerDuty AIOps in the Innovation category, highlighting its dedication to regular product enhancements, increased automation features, and an emphasis on utilizing AI for human-in-the-loop decision-making. This achievement underscores PagerDuty's commitment to staying at the forefront of technological advancements in the industry.

data.title: Federal Reserve governor resigns early, paving way for Trump to appoint new official amid pressure to cut interest rates.
data.description: The recent resignation coincides with President Trump's public calls for the central bank to reduce interest rates and his demand for the chair, Jerome H. Powell, to resign.

data.title: abrdn Income Credit Strategies Fund (NYSE: ACP) launches equity offering program filed with SEC.
data.description: On August 1, 2025, in Philadelphia, the abrdn Income Credit Strategies Fund (NYSE: ACP), a closed-end fund, disclosed that it submitted a prospectus supplement to its registration statement with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

data.title: Hong Kong stablecoin-linked stocks drop by over 10% as per new rules, seen as positive market adjustment.
data.description: Stocks linked to stablecoins in Hong Kong have experienced a significant decline of more than 10% as the city undergoes regulatory changes. Despite this steep drop, analysts view it as a necessary adjustment for the market.

data.title: Kayne Anderson Energy Fund (NYSE: KYN) releases unaudited balance sheet and net asset value as of July 31, 2025.
data.description: On August 1st, 2025, Kayne Anderson Energy Infrastructure Fund, Inc. (NYSE: KYN) released an update on its financial status. The latest unaudited statement of assets and liabilities reveals the company's net asset value and asset coverage ratios in compliance with the Investment Company Act of 1940. This information pertains to the company's standing as of July 31, 2025.
Trump: I have heard that India will no longer be buying oil from Russia.

data.title: BeFra to pay MX $200 million dividend to shareholders, approx US $0.2851 per share before tax, scheduled by August 28, 2025.
data.description: Betterware de México, S.A.P.I. de C.V. (NYSE:BWMX) announced on July 31, 2025, during a shareholders meeting, the approval of an aggregate dividend payment of MX $200,000,000. This equals around US $0.2851 per share before tax withholdings, or about US $0.2566 per share after tax withholdings. The dividend will be disbursed on August 28, 2025, to shareholders of record.

data.title: B&G Foods sells Le Sueur brand to McCall Farms to reduce debt and focus on core brands. "Le Sueur brand has performed very well," says CEO.
data.description: In Parsippany, New Jersey, B&G Foods, Inc. disclosed the sale of the Le Sueur brand, known for premium sweet peas, green beans, and carrots, to McCall Farms, Inc. The sale of the brand, traded under NYSE:BGS, is part of the company's strategy to reshape its portfolio, concentrate on core brands, and lessen long-term debt. Casey Keller, the President and CEO of B&G Foods, mentioned that the Le Sueur brand has been a strong performer in the past year.

data.title: Papa Johns declares $0.46 quarterly dividend per share, equivalent to $1.84 annually, payable on August 29, 2025 (Nasdaq: PZZA).
data.description: Papa John’s International, Inc. (Nasdaq: PZZA) based in Louisville, Kentucky, has announced a quarterly dividend of $0.46 per common share. This dividend will be paid on August 29, 2025, to shareholders holding shares as of August 18, 2025. With this quarterly rate, the annual dividend equals $1.84 per common share. Papa John’s International, Inc. (Nasdaq: PZZA) was established in 1984 with the mission to serve quality pizza.

data.title: "Bogotá leads in foreign tech investment, attracting 60% of FDI in software & IT to Colombia. Report shows city's strong digital economy growth."
data.description: In Bogota, Colombia, on August 1, 2025, over 180 leaders from the business and tech sectors congregated at the "Bogotá: Hub for Technology and Innovation" meeting, hosted by Invest in Bogotá and Michael Page. The gathering aimed to delve into a sector report highlighting Bogotá as the main driver of Colombia's digital economy. The event introduced the report "IT Talent 2025: Investment, Innovation, and Productivity," demonstrating that Bogotá has successfully enticed 60% of all foreign direct investment (FDI) ventures in software and IT that have flowed into Colombia in the last six years. data.title: SSP Innovations and 3-GIS realign for sharper focus, stronger capabilities, and greater value across markets.
data.description: In Denver, SSP and 3-GIS have announced a strategic realignment aimed at enhancing their focus, capabilities, and value delivery within their core markets. This partnership seeks to sharpen their strategic approach and improve their services to customers. This move is set to bring about significant benefits to both companies as they work towards their common goals.
